acceptance_criteria: file runs directly under node with type stripping (no transpile step)
tsc --noEmit reports zero errors for this file
behavior parity: the module's gate command output is unchanged
no silent catch blocks remain in the file
description: Rename tools/install-agent-skills.mjs to .ts and add types using ONLY erasable syntax (type annotations, interfaces, type-only imports — no enums, no namespaces, no parameter properties) so Node type-stripping runs it directly with zero build step. Preserve behavior exactly: same exports, same CLI flags, same output. While converting, audit for silent catches and uninstrumented error paths — fix in place (structured stderr line with area+severity+hint). Update all importers' specifiers in the same change.
title: Convert install-agent-skills.mjs to erasable-syntax TypeScript

acceptance_criteria: gate command exit 0 with output equivalent to pre-conversion run
description: Run the module gate and the tool's own --selftest/--help surface for tools/install-agent-skills.mjs pre- and post-conversion; attach the diff-free evidence to the task. Any intentional output change must be listed explicitly with a reason.
title: Record behavior-parity evidence for install-agent-skills.mjs

acceptance_criteria: plugin installs in one command in a clean profile
description: Create .claude-plugin/plugin.json + marketplace.json bundling the skills (sma-gen3, f5-ultravisionplan, sweetspot-frontend-fix, sweetspot-moa, sma-enforcer, sma-course-builder) with a version-sync script (plugin version = package.json version). Document one-command install in agent-skills/README.md; verify a clean install into a scratch profile loads all skills.
title: Claude plugin packaging for the skill set

description: Create tasklists/skills/_MODULE.md: ownership paths, lane default, gates, graph location, hot-path borders.
title: Write module charter for skills

description: Document skills: responsibility, public seams, how to run/test it locally.
title: Module docs baseline for skills

acceptance_criteria: graph exists and answers a sample ownership question
description: Refresh/create the Graphify module graph for skills so agents can query before broad reads.
title: Graphify module graph for skills

acceptance_criteria: gen3 classifier maps skills paths to skills
description: Add/verify skills module ownership paths in sma.gen3.json; no path overlaps with other modules.
title: Register skills ownership in sma.gen3.json

acceptance_criteria: budget documented and measured in CI
description: Define skills-specific budgets in 07-PERFORMANCE-PLAN.md terms and add the measuring check to CI.
title: Performance budgets for skills

acceptance_criteria: telemetry audit passes for skills paths
description: Audit skills for silent catches and ignored promise failures; wire all real errors into the project telemetry with area/severity/context.
title: Telemetry coverage baseline for skills

acceptance_criteria: module test command exists and runs in CI on affected changes
description: Ensure skills has a runnable module-scoped test command wired into affected-CI so single-module lanes gate locally.
title: Test harness and CI wiring for skills

acceptance_criteria: entry/example exists and the command actually runs
description: Ensure docs/COMMANDS.md has an entry for install-agent-skills.mjs (CLI tools) or an @example doc-comment (lib files): 1-2 line description + one copy-paste command with expected-output snippet, verified against the fixture portfolio where applicable.
title: Add a runnable example for install-agent-skills.mjs to the commands reference

acceptance_criteria: a newcomer can state the file's purpose after reading only the header
description: Top-of-file comment block (6-14 lines) for tools/install-agent-skills.mjs: WHAT it does in one plain sentence, WHY it exists (which real problem/pillar), HOW it fits (inputs, outputs, who calls it), and one copy-paste usage line. Written for a curious newcomer: no acronym without a docs/GLOSSARY.md link, no insider references. Keep the existing first-line shebang/imports untouched.
title: Add a WHY header to install-agent-skills.mjs

acceptance_criteria: the named failure mode has a test that fails before the fix and passes after
description: install-agent-skills and .claude-plugin manifests hardcode skill lists that drift; generate both from a single skills inventory file with a CI check that every skills/* dir is either bundled or explicitly excluded. [P8.5 skeptic finding, 2026-07-10 round 1]
title: Generate installer and plugin manifests from the full skill inventory
